response_text:
# INTERNAL MEMORANDUM

**TO:** All Employees
**FROM:** Sarah Chen, Chief People Officer
**DATE:** June 12, 2025
**RE:** New Flexible Remote Work Policy — Effective July 14, 2025

---

## Overview

After several months of feedback gathering, manager roundtables, and careful review of our productivity data, we are formalizing a new company-wide remote work policy. This memo outlines what the policy includes, why we made these decisions, and what you need to do before it takes effect.

---

## The Policy

**Effective July 14, 2025**, Meridian Software will operate under a **structured hybrid model** with the following terms:

**Core In-Office Days**
All employees are expected to be in the office on **Tuesdays and Thursdays**. These days are mandatory, non-negotiable, and the same across every team. No meetings requiring full-team attendance will be scheduled outside these days.

**Flexible Days**
Monday, Wednesday, and Friday are fully remote. Employees may come into the office on these days if they choose, but there is no obligation to do so.

**Fully Remote Roles**
Approximately 18 roles that were hired as fully remote at the time of offer will remain fully remote. Those employees are not affected by the in-office requirement. If you are unsure whether your role falls into this category, your manager will confirm this with you directly by June 20th.

**Work Hours**
We are maintaining our existing core hours of **10:00 AM – 3:00 PM local time**, during which all employees should be reachable and available for meetings. Outside those hours, flexible scheduling remains in place.

**International and Long-Distance Employees**
Employees living more than 50 miles from our office locations in Austin and Denver are considered remote employees and will follow a modified version of this policy. They are asked to travel to their nearest office location **once per quarter** for a full
